examples.text: The army launched an invasion across the border, with thousands of troops and tanks involved in the offensive.
Soldiers fought a fierce battle as artillery and bombs destroyed the city's defences during the siege.
The general signed a treaty ending the conflict, surrendering the captured territory to enemy forces.
Naval forces engaged in combat at sea, with warships and submarines exchanging fire across the strait.
The military coup overthrew the regime as rebel fighters seized the capital and arrested generals.
An air raid destroyed the bunker, with bomber squadrons targeting munitions factories and weapon depots.
The revolution erupted into civil war as militias and armed factions fought for control of the country.
Troops stormed the fortress, breaching the walls with cannons and capturing prisoners of war.
The physicist published a paper on quantum theory, describing experiments that confirmed his hypothesis about particles.
Scientists discovered a new species of dinosaur, analysing fossils and bones found in the rock formation.
The astronomer observed a comet through the telescope, calculating its orbit and predicting future appearances.
Researchers invented a new vaccine after years of laboratory experiments and clinical trials on patients.
The chemist isolated a new element, studying its atomic structure and chemical properties in the laboratory.
A team of biologists sequenced the genome of the organism, identifying genes responsible for inherited traits.
The engineer designed a steam engine, demonstrating how mechanical work could be produced from heat.
Mathematicians proved a theorem after decades of research, publishing the formal proof in an academic journal.
The expedition mapped uncharted regions, recording new species and collecting biological specimens for study.
The president signed a new law passed by congress, addressing economic policy and government spending priorities.
Voters elected a new prime minister in a landslide, with the ruling party losing seats in parliament.
The senator proposed legislation reforming the tax code, sparking debate among lawmakers and political parties.
The king abdicated the throne, transferring power to his successor in a ceremony attended by ministers.
Diplomats negotiated a trade agreement, signing the treaty after months of talks between governments.
The constitution was ratified, establishing the republic and defining the rights of citizens under federal law.
Protesters demanded democracy and reforms, gathering outside the parliament building to oppose the policy.
The chancellor announced a new cabinet, appointing ministers to oversee finance, foreign affairs, and defence.
The empire annexed the province, expanding its borders through diplomatic and territorial agreements with neighbours.
The team won the championship final, scoring a goal in the last minute to defeat their rivals.
An athlete broke the world record at the Olympics, finishing the race ahead of competitors from many nations.
The boxer knocked out his opponent in the heavyweight title fight, defending his belt for the third time.
The football club signed a star player, paying a record transfer fee for the striker from the rival league.
The tennis player won the grand slam, defeating the seeded champion in straight sets at the tournament.
Cricket fans celebrated a historic test match victory, with the captain leading his team to a series win.
The marathon runner crossed the finish line, claiming gold and setting a new course record at the games.
The cyclist won the tour, completing the final stage and wearing the leader's jersey on the podium.
The pope canonised a new saint, celebrating the ceremony at the basilica with thousands of pilgrims attending.
The temple was consecrated by priests, with worshippers gathering to pray and offer sacrifices to the gods.
The prophet preached to followers, founding a new faith that spread across the kingdom and converted many.
Monks translated the sacred scriptures, copying ancient texts and preserving them in the monastery library.
The cathedral was completed after centuries of construction, becoming the seat of the bishop and archdiocese.
The reformation challenged church doctrine, leading to schism between catholic and protestant denominations.
Pilgrims travelled to the holy city, visiting shrines and observing rituals at the sacred sites of the religion.
The council of bishops convened a synod, debating theological matters and issuing decrees on church teaching.
The composer premiered a new symphony, conducting the orchestra before an audience of music critics and patrons.
The novelist published her debut book, winning literary awards and earning praise from reviewers and readers.
The explorer reached the south pole after months of trekking across the frozen continent in harsh weather.
The painter completed a masterpiece, exhibiting the canvas at the gallery to widespread acclaim from artists.
The architect designed a famous building, planning the structure to blend modern and traditional styles.
The opera singer performed at the royal hall, receiving a standing ovation from the audience and royal family.
The actor starred in a successful film, winning an academy award for his performance in the leading role.
The poet wrote an epic verse, collecting the stanzas into a volume that became a classic of national literature.
The chef opened a celebrated restaurant, serving regional cuisine that earned recognition from food guides.
The philosopher published an influential essay, exploring questions of ethics and human nature for readers.
